mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where UNRATE increases exceed 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean 60-day forward return over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable consumer prices signals healthy capacity utilization without inflationary pressure, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth combined with CPIAUCSL growth below 2% annually yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate inflation hedging and expectations of tighter monetary policy, causing rapid capital outflows from equities and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending power and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of PAYEMS growth above 100k and UNRATE decline fails to produce a positive mean 60-day forward return over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and dividend payout capacity, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during periods of elevated unemployment signal severe economic contraction that forces immediate equity deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth coinciding with UNRATE above 5% yields positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations.
